title: Implementation-phase evidence — commands, exit statuses, and the one routed decision
what: Records the acceptance-sequence runs for the agentic-orchestration advisory reference (matured-approach §8), so The Eye's acceptance rests on reproduced evidence, not assertion.
why: The institution's discipline: local test success does not authorize or merge; the evidence is submitted for TELOS/Eye acceptance.

runs.result: 6 records, taxonomy 5+1, all pinned refs resolve, all ADVISORY, manifest reference-only, README projection equal
215/215 contracts match system reality — addition disturbs no existing normative contract

routed_decisions.outcome: NOT performed this cycle — routed to The Eye
routed_decisions.basis: The manifest's existing index fields are normative_docs (this reference is ADVISORY, not normative) and capability_modules (this reference has no enforcing code, is not a capability module). There is no existing advisory-reference index; adding one is a manifest SCHEMA change. Per matured-approach §1 and CHANGE-PROTOCOL, a schema/boundary change is The Eye's decision — do not invent a second indexing convention. The local checker enforces the negative invariant (the reference is NOT registered as a component/role/future module).
routed_decisions.change_protocol: docs/institutional-memory/CHANGE-PROTOCOL.md (new component boundary / schema change -> human approval)

non_claim: This evidence proves structural integrity, provenance linkage, content-addressing, projection equality, and no-disturbance. It does NOT prove that an advisory pattern choice is optimal or that a worked-example correspondence is semantically complete. Workshop convergence + local test success do not authorize or merge; this is submitted for TELOS/Eye acceptance.
change_rule: Regenerated whenever the record set changes; every listed command must still produce the listed exit status.
